serving.natural_language_portion: One standard grilled chicken wrap weighs approximately 180 g and constitutes a typical single-serve lunch or light meal portion.

nutrient_source.title: Grilled Chicken Wrap — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Grilled Chicken Wrap (cr_id F05020051). All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this record at confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — flour tortilla and mixed chicken wrap analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a grilled chicken wrap as a composite dish. GI estimated at 54 based on the flour tortilla component (GI ~52–58 per University of Sydney GI Database for white flour tortilla) combined with the moderating effect of protein (11.5 g/100g) and fat (10.62 g/100g), which lower the effective glycaemic response. Per the University of Sydney GI classification scale, GI 54 falls in the low category (GI ≤ 55). Glycaemic load per 180 g serving calculated as: (54 × 14.94 g carbohydrate per 100g × 1.8) / 100 ≈ 7.2 GL units. This is a defensible analogous estimate; direct GI testing of this specific product is recommended for higher-confidence classification.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 432 mg per 100 g exceeds the 400 mg/100 g threshold for hypertension concern; a single 180 g wrap delivers approximately 778 mg sodium, representing 34% of the 2300 mg daily limit.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to half a wrap, request no added salt or reduced-sodium sauce, and avoid pairing with other high-sodium condiments such as soy sauce or commercial mayonnaise.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate 14.94 g/100 g with low GI (~54) and low sugar (2.02 g/100 g); protein 11.5 g and fat 10.62 g per 100 g attenuate postprandial glucose rise, but total carbohydrate load per serving requires monitoring.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with type 2 diabetes should choose a wholemeal or high-fibre tortilla to further lower the glycaemic index, avoid sugary sauces, and monitor total carbohydrate intake per serving to stay within their individualised carbohydrate budget.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium 432 mg/100 g and estimated saturated fat ~2.8 g/100 g from the tortilla and sauce are the primary cardiovascular concerns; trace trans fat (~0.1 g/100 g) from the flour tortilla is also a pro-atherogenic factor.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should select a wholegrain tortilla, request a low-sodium sauce, and avoid adding processed cheese or commercial mayonnaise to keep saturated fat and sodium within heart-healthy limits.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Trace trans fat (~0.1 g/100 g from the flour tortilla) is a hard pro-inflammatory trigger for rheumatoid arthritis; refined flour tortilla also contributes low-fibre refined carbohydrates that promote inflammatory mediators.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should substitute a wholegrain or corn tortilla to eliminate trans fat and reduce refined carbohydrate load, and should monitor individual response to tomato as a nightshade vegetable.

llm_training.natural_language_description: A grilled chicken wrap is a Western-style hand-held meal consisting of a flour tortilla filled with grilled chicken breast, mixed vegetables (lettuce, tomato, cabbage), and a sauce. The tortilla is soft and pliable, the chicken is lean and lightly charred from grilling, and the vegetables provide crunch and freshness. The sauce adds moisture and flavour, typically a mayonnaise-based, yoghurt-based, or chilli-based condiment. Per 100 g, the wrap provides 201 kcal, 11.5 g protein, 10.62 g fat, 14.94 g carbohydrate, 2.02 g sugar, 1.75 g dietary fibre, and 432 mg sodium. The protein content is moderate and supports satiety; however, the sodium level at 432 mg/100 g is elevated and is the primary clinical concern for hypertension and cardiovascular disease. The glycaemic index is estimated at approximately 54 (low GI per University of Sydney classification), moderated by the protein and fat content. The wrap is suitable for general healthy eating when portion-controlled but requires sodium reduction for hypertensive individuals and carbohydrate monitoring for those with type 2 diabetes. It is commonly consumed as a lunch or light dinner item and is widely available in cafes, fast-food outlets, and food courts across Singapore and other urban markets. The dish is halal-suitable when prepared with halal-certified chicken and sauce. Dietary tags note that this dish contains gluten from the flour tortilla and may contain soy from the sauce.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F05020051) at confidence 0.95. GI is an analogous estimate based on the University of Sydney GI Database for flour tortilla, adjusted for protein and fat content; this is the best available approved-source estimate for this composite dish. Saturated fat and trans fat values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central data for flour tortilla and grilled chicken components, as the HPB record does not report these sub-fractions.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid sub-fraction data for this composite grilled chicken wrap are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database (F05020051) or the USDA FoodData Central composite dish entry as of 2026-04-16. potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: micronutrient and moisture data for this specific composite dish are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry F05020051 as of 2026-04-16; USDA FoodData Central does not carry an exact composite match. trans_fat_g set to 0.1 g/100 g: estimated trace trans fat from the flour tortilla component based on USDA FoodData Central data for commercially produced flour tortillas; this value is a component-level estimate and not directly reported in the HPB source. saturated_fat_g set to 2.8 g/100 g: estimated from USDA FoodData Central data for grilled chicken breast and flour tortilla components; not directly reported in the HPB source. GI of 54 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database flour tortilla data; per the University of Sydney classification, GI 54 falls in the low category (GI ≤ 55); gi_category corrected from 'medium' to 'low' accordingly; direct GI testing of this product is recommended. Dietary tags in the input incorrectly listed 'vegetarian' and 'plant_based' for a dish containing grilled chicken; these have been corrected to false in dietary_flags. The input cultural.occasion_tags listed 'breakfast' only; meal_context has been expanded to include lunch and light dinner as more representative contexts.
